subject: Product Management
title: Notion
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Notion.

hook.text: Notion's pitch was audacious: replace your docs, wikis, tasks, and databases with one tool. And it worked.

buildup.text: Ivan Zhao believed software tools were too fragmented. People used Docs for writing, Trello for tasks, and Confluence for wikis.

discovery.text: Notion introduced blocks — modular units that could be text, tables, databases, or embeds. Users assembled their own tools.

twist.text: The company nearly died twice. After rebuilding from scratch in Japan, it found PMF with a niche of power users.

climax.text: Notion grew entirely through word of mouth and templates shared by the community — zero paid marketing to $10 billion valuation.

punchline.text: Give users building blocks. They'll build tools you never imagined.

quiz.question: What was Notion's key product innovation?
options: Modular blocks that let users assemble their own tools — replacing multiple apps
Being the cheapest alternative to Microsoft Office
